title: Writing - Part 2
description: Choose one of the following writing tasks and write your answer in 140-190 words.

tasks.title: Review
tasks.prompt:
You see this notice on a website:

'Have you seen a film recently that you would recommend to other English learners? Write a review describing the film, saying what you liked or disliked about it, and whether you would recommend it.'

Write your review.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Film Review: Everything Everywhere All at Once

Everything Everywhere All at Once is an American science-fiction comedy that has become one of the most talked-about films of recent years. Directed by the Daniels, it follows a middle-aged Chinese-American woman who must connect with parallel versions of herself to save the universe.

What makes this film truly remarkable is how it balances wild, imaginative humour with genuinely moving emotional moments. The performances are outstanding, particularly Michelle Yeoh, who brings extraordinary depth to her role. The film is visually creative and unlike anything else in mainstream cinema.

However, the rapid pace and complex plot may feel overwhelming on a first viewing. I would suggest watching with subtitles, as some sections are deliberately chaotic.

For English learners, this film is excellent because the dialogue is natural and varied, ranging from formal to very informal speech. It is funny, original, and deeply human.

Overall rating: 5/5 — an unforgettable experience that I wholeheartedly recommend.

tasks.title: Email
tasks.prompt:
Your English friend Jordan has invited you to join a film club that meets every month to discuss a chosen film. Write an email to Jordan saying whether you would like to join, asking about how the club works, and suggesting a film for the first meeting.

Write your email to Jordan.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Hi Jordan,

What a great idea! I would absolutely love to join the film club — it sounds like exactly the kind of thing I've been looking for. I've been meaning to watch more films and discuss them properly rather than just enjoying them alone.

Could you tell me a bit more about how it works? For example, does everyone watch the film separately beforehand, or do you watch it together? And how long do the discussions usually last? I'd also like to know if there's a membership fee or any other commitment involved.

As for the first meeting, I'd love to suggest Parasite, the South Korean film by Bong Joon-ho. It won the Academy Award for Best Picture a few years ago and it's absolutely brilliant. The themes of class and inequality make for fascinating discussion, and the plot twists are genuinely shocking.

I'm really looking forward to hearing more about it!

Best wishes,
Cameron

tasks.title: Article
tasks.prompt:
You see this notice in an English-language magazine:

'We want to know: are streaming services killing the cinema experience? Write an article sharing your views on whether going to the cinema is still worth it in the age of Netflix and online streaming.'

Write your article.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Lights, Camera, Debate: Is Cinema Still Worth It?

With Netflix, Disney+, and countless other platforms offering thousands of films at the touch of a button, many people are asking whether the traditional cinema experience is becoming obsolete. I believe it is not — but it does need to evolve.

There is something irreplaceable about watching a film on a huge screen with powerful sound. Epic action scenes, sweeping landscapes, and emotional climaxes simply hit differently in a darkened cinema hall. No home television, however large, can fully replicate that impact.

That said, streaming has clear advantages. It is cheaper, more convenient, and allows viewers to pause, rewind, and watch on their own schedule. For everyday viewing, it is hard to beat.

The solution, I think, is for cinemas to specialise. Rather than competing with streaming on quantity, they should focus on offering an event experience — premium seats, restaurant-quality food, and exclusive early releases.

Cinema is not dying. It is simply changing — and that is perfectly fine.
